claim: The binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML2 is similar to the binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML1.

result.claim: The binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML2 is similar to the binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML1.
result.reasoning: A thorough search for propositions and document chunks related to the binding orientation of ML-SA1 at hTRPML1 and hTRPML2, and a comparison between them, yielded no relevant results. The search queries included "The binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML2 is similar to the binding orientation of the ML-SA1 activator at hTRPML1", "ML-SA1 activator hTRPML2 binding orientation", "ML-SA1 activator hTRPML1 binding orientation", "ML-SA1 hTRPML1 binding", and "ML-SA1 hTRPML2 binding". The retrieved propositions and chunks were about various other binding proteins, activators, or general binding site information, but none specifically addressed the binding orientation of ML-SA1 at either hTRPML1 or hTRPML2, nor did they provide any comparative information. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to support or refute the claim.
